title: symplocos crataegoides var. glabra
intro: Symplocos crataegoides var. glabra, also known as the smooth Indian alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Symplocos crataegoides var. glabra include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
